slides.title: Vocabulary: Old Objects
slides.content:
Let's learn words about objects from the past:

🕰️ clock — a machine that tells the time

💡 lamp — gives light (old lamps used oil, not electricity!)

🪑 chair — a seat with a back

🧥 bathrobe — a soft coat you wear at home after a bath

👔 suit — smart clothes (jacket and trousers)

🏛️ museum — a place where you can see old and interesting things

slides.title: More Past Tense Verbs
slides.content:
Let's practise more regular verbs in the Simple Past:

arrive → arrived (Grandpa arrived at school early.)

clean → cleaned (He cleaned the house.)

climb → climbed (He climbed trees.)

check → checked (He checked the clock.)

sail → sailed (Grandma sailed a boat.)

ask → asked (Emma asked a question.)
slides.tip: All these verbs are regular: just add -ed (or -d if they end in 'e').

slides.title: Grandpa's Story
slides.html:
'Grandpa, tell me a story about when you were young!' said Emma.

Grandpa smiled. 'When I was a boy, I arrived at school very early every morning. I walked two kilometres because there were no buses.'

'That is far!' said Emma.

'After school, I cleaned the house and visited my grandmother. She sailed a little boat on the river every day.'

'What about toys?' asked Emma.

'We played outside. I climbed trees and checked the clock to make sure I was home before dark.'

slides.title: What Did They Do?
slides.problem:
Put these verbs in the past tense:

1. Grandpa (arrive) at school early.
2. He (clean) the house after school.
3. Grandma (sail) a boat on the river.
4. Emma (ask) about toys.
slides.answer: 1. Grandpa arrived at school early. 2. He cleaned the house. 3. Grandma sailed a boat. 4. Emma asked about toys.

slides.title: At the Museum
slides.content:
A museum is a place where we can see things from the past.

In a museum you can see:

🕰️ Old clocks — they were very big!

💡 Old lamps — they used oil, not electricity.

🪑 Old chairs — people sat on chairs like these long ago.

👔 Old suits and bathrobes — clothes were different!

Museums help us learn about how people lived in the past.

slides.title: A Class Trip to the Museum
slides.html:
Today the class visited a museum about life long ago.

'Look at this old clock!' said Tom. 'It is very big!'

'And look at this old chair!' said Sara. 'People sat on chairs like this one.'

They saw an old lamp. It used oil, not electricity! They also saw an old bathrobe and a beautiful suit from a hundred years ago.

The teacher asked: 'How did people travel a hundred years ago?'

'They walked or used horses,' said Lucy.

'People arrived at places very slowly. Now we can travel fast!'

slides.title: Finding the Past Tense
slides.content:
How do you know a verb is in the past tense?

Look for the -ed ending!

✅ walked, played, cleaned → all past tense

Look for time words that tell you it is the past:

📅 yesterday, last week, long ago, when I was young

Example: Yesterday, I climbed a tree.
slides.tip: If you hear or see -ed at the end of a verb, it is probably in the past tense!

slides.title: Past Tense Story
slides.problem:
Complete the story with the past form of the verbs:

Last weekend, we (visit) ___ Grandma. She (cook) ___ a big lunch. After lunch, I (climb) ___ a tree and my brother (play) ___ with the dog. We (check) ___ the time and (walk) ___ home.
slides.answer: Last weekend, we visited Grandma. She cooked a big lunch. I climbed a tree and my brother played with the dog. We checked the time and walked home.

slides.title: How to Pronounce -ed
slides.content:
The -ed ending has three different sounds:

🔹 /t/ — after voiceless sounds (k, p, s, sh):
walked, asked, checked

🔹 /d/ — after voiced sounds (l, n, v, m, r):
climbed, cleaned, sailed

🔹 /ɪd/ — after 't' or 'd' sounds:
visited, arrived
slides.tip: Say the words out loud. Can you hear the difference? 'walked' (t), 'cleaned' (d), 'visited' (id).

slides.title: Grandma's Favourite Day
slides.html:
Emma asked Grandma about her favourite day when she was young.

'One summer day, I sailed on a boat with my father,' said Grandma. 'We arrived at a beautiful island.'

'We climbed a small mountain and looked at the sea. Then we walked on the beach and played in the water.'

'In the evening, my father checked the clock and said it was time to go home. I cleaned the sand off my feet and we sailed back.'

'It was the best day ever!' said Grandma with a big smile.

slides.title: Let's Review!
points: 🕰️ Vocabulary: clock, lamp, chair, bathrobe, suit, museum
📝 More past tense verbs: arrived, cleaned, climbed, checked, sailed, asked
🔤 Regular verbs: add -ed (or -d if the verb ends in 'e')
🔍 Look for -ed endings and time words (yesterday, long ago) to spot the past
🏛️ Museums help us learn about how people lived in the past
